metadata.description: Complete biblical genealogy from Adam to Jesus with dual-calendar dating (Zadok Year 0 = 4004 BC), relational data, lineage tracking, and classification system
metadata.calendar_info: Zadok Year 0 corresponds to -4004 Gregorian (4004 BC). Add 4004 to Zadok year to get Gregorian year.

seth_line.name: Line of Seth (The Godly Line)
seth_line.description: The line through which God's covenant was preserved through the Flood to Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and ultimately Jesus

jesus_line.name: Jesus's Genealogy
jesus_line.description: Direct line from Adam through Seth, Noah, Shem, Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, Judah, David to Jesus

cain_line.name: Line of Cain (The Ungodly Line)
cain_line.description: Cain's descendants, representing human civilization apart from God, judged in the Flood

ham_line.name: Line of Ham (Noahic Covenant)
ham_line.description: Post-Flood lineage of Canaan, representing nations under the Noahic covenant

people.name: Adam
people.title: First Man, Father of All

major_events.description: Adam created in God's image (Genesis 1:27)
Placed in Garden, given dominion (Genesis 2:15)
Ate forbidden fruit, sin entered world (Genesis 3:1-6)
First son born after Fall (Genesis 4:1)
Second son, became shepherd (Genesis 4:2)
Third son, appointed to replace Abel (Genesis 4:25)
Lived 930 years (Genesis 5:5)

people.name: Eve
people.title: First Woman, Mother of All

major_events.description: Created from Adam's rib (Genesis 2:22)
Deceived by serpent, ate forbidden fruit (Genesis 3:1-6)

people.name: Cain
people.title: First Son, First Murderer

major_events.description: First-born son (Genesis 4:1)
Killed brother in jealousy (Genesis 4:8)
Marked by God, banished east of Eden (Genesis 4:15)

people.name: Abel
people.title: First Shepherd, First Martyr

major_events.description: Second son (Genesis 4:2)
Brought firstlings of flock; accepted by God (Genesis 4:4)
Killed by brother Cain (Genesis 4:8)

people.name: Seth
people.title: Third Son, Covenant Heir

major_events.description: Born to replace Abel (Genesis 4:25)
Lived 912 years (Genesis 5:8)

people.name: Enosh
people.title: Son of Seth
tags: When men began calling on Lord

major_events.description: Son of Seth (Genesis 5:6)
In his day men began calling on name of Lord (Genesis 4:26)
Lived 905 years (Genesis 5:11)

people.name: Cainan
people.title: Son of Enosh

major_events.description: Son of Enosh (Genesis 5:9)
Lived 910 years (Genesis 5:14)

people.name: Mahalalel
people.title: Son of Cainan

major_events.description: Son of Cainan (Genesis 5:12)
Lived 895 years (Genesis 5:17)

people.name: Jared
people.title: Son of Mahalalel, Longest-Lived

major_events.description: Son of Mahalalel (Genesis 5:15)
Lived 962 years—longest lifespan in Genesis (Genesis 5:20)

people.name: Enoch
people.title: Son of Jared, Walked with God

major_events.description: Son of Jared (Genesis 5:18)
Lived in close fellowship with God (Genesis 5:22-24)
Prophesied judgment (Jude 1:14)
Taken by God without experiencing death (Genesis 5:24, Hebrews 11:5)

people.name: Methuselah
people.title: Son of Enoch, Oldest Person

major_events.description: Son of Enoch (Genesis 5:21)
Lived 969 years—oldest person ever (Genesis 5:27)
Died the same year as the Great Flood

people.name: Lamech
people.title: Son of Methuselah, Noah's Father

major_events.description: Son of Methuselah (Genesis 5:25)
Fathered Noah; named him expressing hope (Genesis 5:29)
Lived 777 years (Genesis 5:31)

people.name: Noah
people.title: Righteous Man, Ark Builder, Flood Survivor

major_events.description: Son of Lamech (Genesis 5:29)
Preached for 120 years before Flood (2 Peter 2:5)
Built ark for 120 years (Genesis 6:14)
Entered ark; all life destroyed except those in ark (Genesis 7:11-12)
Received Noahic Covenant with rainbow sign (Genesis 8:20-9:17)
Lived 950 years (Genesis 9:29)

people.name: Shem
people.title: Son of Noah, Blessed Son

major_events.description: Son of Noah (Genesis 5:32)
Entered ark with family (Genesis 7:7)
Blessed by Noah; promised Canaan (Genesis 9:26-27)
Lived 600 years (Genesis 11:11)

people.name: Arphaxad
people.title: Son of Shem

major_events.description: Son of Shem, born after Flood (Genesis 11:10)
Lived 438 years (Genesis 11:13)

people.name: Shelah
people.title: Son of Arphaxad

major_events.description: Son of Arphaxad (Genesis 11:14)
Lived 433 years (Genesis 11:15)

people.name: Eber
people.title: Son of Shelah, Hebrew Ancestor

major_events.description: Son of Shelah (Genesis 11:16)
Lived 464 years (Genesis 11:17)

people.name: Peleg
people.title: Son of Eber, Tower of Babel Era

major_events.description: Son of Eber; name means 'division' (Genesis 11:18)
Lived during division of languages at Babel (Genesis 11:9)
Lived 239 years (Genesis 11:19)

people.name: Reu
people.title: Son of Peleg

major_events.description: Son of Peleg (Genesis 11:20)
Lived 239 years (Genesis 11:21)

people.name: Serug
people.title: Son of Reu

major_events.description: Son of Reu (Genesis 11:22)
Lived 230 years (Genesis 11:23)

people.name: Nahor
people.title: Son of Serug, Abraham's Grandfather

major_events.description: Son of Serug (Genesis 11:24)
Lived 148 years (Genesis 11:25)

people.name: Terah
people.title: Son of Nahor, Abraham's Father

major_events.description: Son of Nahor (Genesis 11:26)
Fathered Abraham (Genesis 11:26)
Left Ur for Canaan but stopped in Haran (Genesis 11:31)
Died in Haran, lived 205 years (Genesis 11:32)

people.name: Abraham
people.title: Father of Nations, Covenant Patriarch

major_events.description: Son of Terah (Genesis 11:26)
God called Abram to leave homeland (Genesis 12:1)
Made covenant with Abram, promised offspring (Genesis 12:2-3)
Ishmael born to Hagar (Genesis 16:16)
Abram's name changed to Abraham; covenant renewed (Genesis 17:5)
Isaac born to Sarah at age 100 (Genesis 21:5)
Abraham tested; offered Isaac; type of crucifixion (Genesis 22:1-19)
Died and was gathered to his people, lived 175 years (Genesis 25:7-8)

people.name: Sarah
people.title: Wife of Abraham, Mother of Isaac

major_events.description: Journeyed with Abraham from Ur to Canaan
Bore Isaac at age 90, fulfilling covenant (Genesis 21:2)
Died in Hebron, lived 127 years (Genesis 23:1)

people.name: Isaac
people.title: Son of Promise, Covenant Heir

major_events.description: Born to Abraham and Sarah (Genesis 21:1-3)
Offered as sacrifice by Abraham; type of resurrection (Genesis 22:1-19)
Took Rebekah as bride; loved her (Genesis 24:67)
Twin sons born; Jacob received the birthright (Genesis 25:24-34)
Died in Mamre at age 180 (Genesis 35:28-29)

people.name: Rebekah
people.title: Wife of Isaac, Mother of Jacob

major_events.description: Became Isaac's wife (Genesis 24:51-67)
Bore Jacob and Esau (Genesis 25:21-26)

people.name: Jacob (Israel)
people.title: Son of Isaac, Father of Twelve Tribes

major_events.description: Twin son of Isaac; obtained birthright (Genesis 25:24-34)
Saw ladder to heaven; covenant renewed (Genesis 28:10-22)
Served Laban 7 years for Rachel (Genesis 29:18-30)
Wrestled with God; name changed to Israel (Genesis 32:24-32)
Reconciliation with brother Esau (Genesis 33:1-15)
Died in Egypt; lived 147 years (Genesis 47:28)

people.name: Rachel
people.title: Wife of Jacob, Mother of Joseph and Benjamin

major_events.description: Became Jacob's wife after 7 years service (Genesis 29:18-30)
Bore Joseph after years of barrenness (Genesis 30:22-24)
Died giving birth to Benjamin (Genesis 35:16-19)

people.name: Judah
people.title: Son of Jacob, Father of Judean Line

major_events.description: Fourth son of Jacob; praise given to God (Genesis 29:35)
Fathered Perez through Tamar (Genesis 38:1-30)
Received scepter promise; Messiah foretold (Genesis 49:8-12)

people.name: Leah
people.title: Wife of Jacob, Mother of Judah

major_events.description: Given to Jacob instead of Rachel (Genesis 29:16-30)
Bore the son through whom Messiah came

people.name: Perez
people.title: Son of Judah, Ancestor of David

major_events.description: Twin son born to Judah and Tamar (Genesis 38:27-30)
Went to Egypt with Jacob and family (Genesis 46:12)

people.name: Tamar
people.title: Wife of Er and Judah, Mother of Perez

major_events.description: Married Er, Judah's firstborn (Genesis 38:6)
Secured her right through Judah; bore Perez (Genesis 38:1-30)

people.name: Hezron
people.title: Son of Perez, Founder of Judean Houses

major_events.description: Son of Perez, born in Egypt
Lived during Israel's time in Egypt (Exodus 6:15)

people.name: Ram
people.title: Son of Hezron, Ancestor of David

major_events.description: Son of Hezron (Ruth 4:19, Matthew 1:3)

people.name: Amminadab
people.title: Son of Ram, Prince of Judah

major_events.description: Son of Ram
Led Judah in wilderness (Numbers 1:7)

people.name: Nahshon
people.title: Son of Amminadab, Prince of Judah

major_events.description: Son of Amminadab
Prince of Judah; led people in wilderness (Numbers 1:7, 7:12)
Married Aaron's sister (Exodus 6:23)

people.name: Elisheba
people.title: Wife of Nahshon, Sister of Aaron

major_events.description: Became wife of Judah's prince (Exodus 6:23)

people.name: Salmon
people.title: Son of Nahshon, Father of Boaz

major_events.description: Son of Nahshon
Married Canaanite woman redeemed through faith (Joshua 2, 6:22-25)

people.name: Rahab
people.title: Woman of Jericho, Mother of Boaz

major_events.description: Protected Hebrew spies; demonstrated faith in God (Joshua 2:1-21)
Saved alive from destruction; entered Israel (Joshua 6:22-25)
Married prince of Judah (Matthew 1:5)

people.name: Boaz
people.title: Mighty Man of Valor, Kinsman Redeemer

major_events.description: Son of Salmon and Rahab
Observed Ruth gleaning; showed kindness (Ruth 2:1-13)
Redeemed Ruth as kinsman redeemer; type of Christ (Ruth 3-4)
Married Ruth and continued Judean line (Ruth 4:10-13)

people.name: Ruth
people.title: Woman of Moab, Ancestress of David and Jesus

major_events.description: Refused to leave Naomi; committed to Israel and God (Ruth 1:16-17)
Worked in Boaz's field; found favor (Ruth 2:1-13)
Redeemed by Boaz; became wife and mother of David's line (Ruth 3-4)

people.name: Obed
people.title: Son of Boaz and Ruth, Father of Jesse

major_events.description: Son of Boaz and Ruth (Ruth 4:17)
Fulfilled kinsman redeemer role; perpetuated David's line (Ruth 4:17)

people.name: Jesse
people.title: Father of David, Root of Jesse

major_events.description: Son of Obed
Fathered David, youngest son (1 Samuel 16:10-13)
Samuel anointed David as king in Jesse's house (1 Samuel 16:1-13)

people.name: David
people.title: King of Israel, Man After God's Heart, Ancestor of Jesus
people.prophetic_role: King, Psalmist, Messiah's Prototype, Covenant Head

major_events.description: Youngest son of Jesse, born in Bethlehem (1 Samuel 16:1-13)
Samuel anointed David; Spirit came upon him (1 Samuel 16:1-13)
Defeated Philistine giant with faith in God (1 Samuel 17:1-54)
Bond with Saul's son; covenant of love (1 Samuel 18:1-4)
Fled from Saul for approximately 10 years
Became king of Judah at age 30; later king of all Israel (2 Samuel 2:1-7, 5:1-5)
Made Jerusalem capital; brought Ark of Covenant (2 Samuel 5:6-12, 6:1-19)
Covenant: throne established forever; Messiah from his line (2 Samuel 7:1-17)
Took Bathsheba; born son Solomon who continued throne (2 Samuel 11-12)
Wrote Psalms; prophesied about Messiah (e.g., Psalm 22, 110)
Died at age 70; buried in City of David (1 Kings 2:10)

people.name: Bathsheba
people.title: Wife of David, Mother of Solomon

major_events.description: Became David's wife; mother of Solomon (2 Samuel 11-12)
Bore Solomon, heir to David's throne (2 Samuel 12:25)

people.name: Solomon
people.title: King of Israel, Builder of Temple, Wisdom
people.prophetic_role: Wise King, Temple Builder, Messianic Type

major_events.description: Second son of David and Bathsheba (2 Samuel 12:25)
God appeared to Solomon; loved by God (2 Samuel 12:25)
Became king after David's death at age 20 (1 Kings 1:11-48)
Asked God for wisdom; granted both wisdom and riches (1 Kings 3:4-15)
Built magnificent Temple in Jerusalem; took 7 years (1 Kings 5-8)
Dedicated Temple; God's glory filled it (1 Kings 8:1-66)
Kingdom at peace; wisdom renowned throughout world (1 Kings 4:29-34)
Died; reigned 40 years (1 Kings 11:42)

people.name: Rehoboam
people.title: King of Judah, Son of Solomon

major_events.description: Son of Solomon and Naamah the Ammonitess
Became king of Judah; took foolish counsel (1 Kings 12:1-20)
Northern tribes rebelled; formed Israel under Jeroboam (1 Kings 12:16-33)
Reigned 17 years over Judah (1 Kings 14:21)

people.name: Abijah
people.title: King of Judah, Son of Rehoboam

major_events.description: Became king of Judah (1 Kings 14:31, 15:1)
Fought Jeroboam; God delivered Judah (2 Chronicles 13:1-20)

people.name: Asa
people.title: King of Judah, Reformer

major_events.description: Became king at age 20 (1 Kings 15:9-11)
Removed idols and high places; restored worship (2 Chronicles 14:2-5)
Defeated great army; miracle from God (2 Chronicles 14:9-12)

people.name: Jehoshaphat
people.title: King of Judah, Judge and Reformer

major_events.description: Became king of Judah (1 Kings 15:24)
Sent judges throughout Judah (2 Chronicles 17:7-9)

people.name: Jehoram
people.title: King of Judah, Son of Jehoshaphat

major_events.description: Became king of Judah (2 Kings 8:16)

people.name: Ahaziah
people.title: King of Judah, Son of Jehoram

major_events.description: Became king at age 22 (2 Kings 8:26)
Killed by Jehu; reigned only 1 year (2 Kings 9:24-28)

people.name: Joash
people.title: King of Judah, Child King

major_events.description: Became king at age 7; hidden from Athaliah (2 Kings 11:1-12)
Repaired the Temple of God (2 Kings 12:4-16)

people.name: Amaziah
people.title: King of Judah, Son of Joash

major_events.description: Became king of Judah (2 Kings 14:1)

people.name: Uzziah (Azariah)
people.title: King of Judah, Powerful King

major_events.description: Became king at age 16 (2 Kings 15:1-3)
Defeated Philistines, Arabs, Meunites (2 Chronicles 26:6-8)

people.name: Jotham
people.title: King of Judah, Righteous King

major_events.description: Became king of Judah (2 Kings 15:32-35)

people.name: Ahaz
people.title: King of Judah, Unfaithful King

major_events.description: Became king of Judah (2 Kings 16:1-2)
Isaiah prophesied of Immanuel; sign: virgin will conceive (Isaiah 7:10-14)

people.name: Hezekiah
people.title: King of Judah, Reformer and Healer

major_events.description: Became king of Judah at age 25 (2 Kings 18:1-2)
Removed idols and high places; cleansed Temple (2 Kings 18:4)
Assyrian army defeated by angel of God (2 Kings 19:35-36, Isaiah 36-37)
Healed of terminal illness; life extended 15 years (2 Kings 20:1-11)

people.name: Manasseh
people.title: King of Judah, Wicked Then Repentant

major_events.description: Became king at age 12 (2 Kings 21:1)
Did evil; idolatry and wickedness (2 Kings 21:1-18)
Captured by Assyrians; repented; God restored him (2 Chronicles 33:10-13)

people.name: Amon
people.title: King of Judah, Wicked King

major_events.description: Became king at age 22 (2 Kings 21:19)
Reigned only 2 years; murdered by servants (2 Kings 21:19-26)

people.name: Josiah
people.title: King of Judah, Great Reformer

major_events.description: Became king at age 8; did right (2 Kings 22:1)
Book of the Law discovered during Temple repairs (2 Kings 22:8)
Removed all idols; kept Passover (2 Kings 23:1-30)
Died fighting Egyptian Pharaoh Necho (2 Kings 23:29)

people.name: Jehoiakim
people.title: King of Judah, Evil King, Pre-Exile

major_events.description: Made king by Pharaoh Necho; renamed from Eliakim (2 Kings 23:34)
Babylon besieged Jerusalem; became vassal (2 Kings 24:1-6)

people.name: Jehoiachin (Coniah)
people.title: King of Judah, Exiled King
people.prophetic_role: Last Davidic King in Jerusalem, Exiled

major_events.description: Became king at age 18; reigned only 3 months (2 Kings 24:8)
Captured by Nebuchadnezzar; exiled to Babylon (2 Kings 24:12-16, 2 Chronicles 36:9-10)
Released by Evil-Merodach; given honor (2 Kings 25:27-30)

people.name: Shealtiel
people.title: Son of Jehoiachin, Father of Zerubbabel

major_events.description: Born in Babylon during exile (1 Chronicles 3:17)

people.name: Zerubbabel
people.title: Governor of Judah, Temple Rebuilder

major_events.description: Led first group of exiles back to Judah under Cyrus's decree (Ezra 1:8, 2:2)
Laid foundation of second Temple (Ezra 3:8-13)
Second Temple completed and dedicated (Ezra 6:14-18)

people.name: Abiud
people.title: Son of Zerubbabel

major_events.description: Son of Zerubbabel (Matthew 1:13)

people.name: Eliakim
people.title: Son of Abiud

major_events.description: Son of Abiud (Matthew 1:13)

people.name: Azor
people.title: Son of Eliakim

major_events.description: Son of Azor (Matthew 1:13-14)

people.name: Sadoc
people.title: Son of Azor

major_events.description: Son of Sadoc (Matthew 1:14)

people.name: Achim
people.title: Son of Sadoc

major_events.description: Son of Achim (Matthew 1:14)

people.name: Eliud
people.title: Son of Achim

major_events.description: Son of Eliud (Matthew 1:15)

people.name: Eleazar
people.title: Son of Eliud

major_events.description: Son of Eleazar (Matthew 1:15)

people.name: Matthan
people.title: Son of Eleazar, Grandfather of Joseph

major_events.description: Son of Matthan (Matthew 1:15)

people.name: Jacob
people.title: Son of Matthan, Father of Joseph

major_events.description: Son of Jacob (Matthew 1:16)

people.name: Joseph
people.title: Earthly Father of Jesus, Carpenter of Nazareth

major_events.description: Son of Jacob; carpenter (Matthew 1:16, 13:55)
Betrothed to Mary of Nazareth (Matthew 1:18)
Angel revealed Mary's pregnancy; told him Jesus's purpose (Matthew 1:20-21)
Witnessed birth of Jesus in Bethlehem; raised him (Matthew 1:25, Luke 2:1-7)
Fled with Jesus to Egypt to escape Herod (Matthew 2:13-15)
Settled in Nazareth; raised Jesus in carpenter's trade (Matthew 2:19-23)

people.name: Mary (Miriam)
people.title: Virgin of Nazareth, Mother of Jesus

major_events.description: Born in Nazareth or Jerusalem
Angel Gabriel announced she would bear the Son of God (Luke 1:26-38)
Jesus conceived by Holy Spirit; Mary found with child (Matthew 1:18, Luke 1:26-35)
Gave birth to Jesus in Bethlehem (Matthew 2:1-7, Luke 2:1-20)
Presented Jesus in Temple; prophecy of sword piercing soul (Luke 2:22-35)
Fled with Jesus and Joseph to Egypt (Matthew 2:13-15)
Witnessed Jesus's crucifixion; entrusted to John (John 19:25-27)

people.name: Jesus
people.title: The Messiah, Son of God, Son of Man, Savior of the World
people.prophetic_role: Messiah, Lamb of God, Redeemer, Savior

major_events.description: Conceived by Holy Spirit in womb of virgin Mary (Matthew 1:18-25, Luke 1:26-38, Isaiah 7:14)
Born in Bethlehem; fulfillment of Micah 5:2 (Matthew 2:1-7, Luke 2:1-20)
Magi visited bearing gifts; Jesus presented as King to nations (Matthew 2:1-12)
Circumcised on eighth day; named Jesus (Luke 2:21)
Presented as firstborn; recognized as Light of Gentiles (Luke 2:22-35)
Family fled to Egypt to escape Herod's massacre (Matthew 2:13-15, Hosea 11:1)
Grew up in Nazareth; trained in carpenter's trade (Matthew 2:19-23, Luke 2:39-40, 52)
Found in Temple discussing with teachers; demonstrated wisdom (Luke 2:41-50)
Baptized in Jordan; Spirit descended as dove; Father's voice confirmed (Matthew 3:13-17, Mark 1:9-11, Luke 3:21-22)
Tempted by Satan for 40 days; overcome through God's word (Matthew 4:1-11, Mark 1:12-13, Luke 4:1-13)
Called Simon Peter, Andrew, James, John as disciples (John 1:35-51)
Began public ministry with signs and teaching; proclaimed Kingdom (John 2-4)
Preached blessings and teachings of Kingdom (Matthew 5-7)
Cast out demons, healed sick, raised dead, calmed storms (Matthew 8-9, Mark 1-5, Luke 5-9)
Selected 12 apostles as foundation of Church (Matthew 10:1-4, Mark 3:13-19, Luke 6:12-16)
Revealed glory before Peter, James, John; Law and Prophets testified (Matthew 17:1-8, Mark 9:2-8, Luke 9:28-36)
Taught in parables about Kingdom; ultimate authority revealed (Matthew 13, 18-25, Mark 4, 10-14, Luke 8, 15-18)
Entered Jerusalem on donkey; hailed as Messiah (Matthew 21:1-11, Mark 11:1-11, Luke 19:28-40, John 12:12-15, Zechariah 9:9)
Overturned tables of money changers; demonstrated authority (Matthew 21:12-17, Mark 11:15-19, Luke 19:45-48)
Instituted covenant meal; broke bread and cup; 'This is my body, this is my blood' (Matthew 26:17-30, Mark 14:12-26, Luke 22:7-23, 1 Corinthians 11:23-26)
Prayed 'Not my will but yours be done'; sweated drops of blood (Matthew 26:36-46, Mark 14:32-42, Luke 22:39-46)
Betrayed by Judas; arrested by officers of Sanhedrin (Matthew 26:47-56, Mark 14:43-52, Luke 22:47-53, John 18:2-12)
Questioned about being Messiah; condemned for blasphemy (Matthew 26:57-68, Mark 14:53-65, Luke 22:54-71, John 18:12-27)
Brought before Roman governor; accused of sedition; Pilate found no fault (Matthew 27:1-26, Mark 15:1-15, Luke 23:1-25, John 18:28-19:16)
Beaten by Roman soldiers; crowned with thorns; mocked as King (Matthew 27:26-31, Mark 15:15-20, John 19:1-5, Isaiah 50:6, 52:14)
Crucified at Golgotha between two thieves; darkness over land; veil torn (Matthew 27:32-56, Mark 15:21-41, Luke 23:26-49, John 19:17-37, Psalm 22, Isaiah 53)
Side pierced by soldier's spear; blood and water flowed (John 19:34, Zechariah 12:10)
Buried in new tomb of Joseph of Arimathea; stone sealed (Matthew 27:57-66, Mark 15:42-47, Luke 23:50-56, John 19:38-42, Isaiah 53:9)
Rose on the third day; death conquered; firstfruits of resurrection (Matthew 28:1-10, Mark 16:1-8, Luke 24:1-12, John 20:1-18, 1 Corinthians 15:3-8)
Appeared to disciples, Mary, Peter, 500+ believers over 40 days (Matthew 28:16-20, Luke 24:36-43, John 20-21, 1 Corinthians 15:3-8)
Ascended to heaven 40 days after resurrection; seated at Father's right hand (Matthew 28:19-20, Mark 16:19, Luke 24:50-53, Acts 1:9-11, Hebrews 10:12-13)
Exalted; all authority given; intercedes for saints; awaits return (Philippians 2:9-11, Colossians 3:1, Hebrews 1:3, 10:12-13, Revelation 3:21)

jesus_lineage.description: Direct patrilineal descent from Adam to Jesus through Matthew's gospel
jesus_lineage.note: Path: Adam → Seth → ... → Abraham → Isaac → Jacob → Judah → Perez → Hezron → Ram → Amminadab → Nahshon → Salmon → Boaz → Obed → Jesse → David → Solomon → Rehoboam → ... → Jesus (Matthew 1:1-17, Luke 3:23-38)

davidic_covenant.description: The covenant established with David that his throne would be established forever, fulfilled in Jesus

kinsman_redeemer_type.description: Boaz as a type of Christ, redeeming Ruth as Christ redeems the church
